annotations.label: Pigeonhole principle (basic statement)

anchor.snippet: at least one container must contain more than one item

annotations.note: Verified: states that if card β < card α then any f : α → β has two distinct inputs with equal output.

annotations.label: Three gloves handedness

anchor.snippet: at least two must be right-handed or at least two must be left-handed

annotations.note: This specific worked example is not formalized, but it is a direct invocation of the finite pigeonhole principle (3 gloves, 2 handednesses).

annotations.label: Two Londoners with same hair count

anchor.snippet: there must be at least two people in London who have the same number of hairs on their heads

annotations.note: Illustrative example not separately formalized; it is an application of the finite pigeonhole principle.

annotations.label: Quantified pigeonhole principle

anchor.snippet: at least one of the sets will contain at least k + 1 objects

annotations.note: Verified: if card β * n < card α then some fiber has more than n elements, i.e. ≥ k+1 when count exceeds kn.

annotations.label: Formal statement (injective function)

anchor.snippet: there does not exist an injective function whose codomain is smaller than its domain

annotations.note: Verified: if card β < card α then α ↪ β is empty; equivalently Fintype.card_le_of_injective gives the contrapositive.

annotations.label: Leurechon hairs statement

anchor.snippet: It is necessary that two men have the same number of hairs

annotations.note: Historical illustrative statement not formalized as such; it is an instance of the finite pigeonhole principle.

annotations.label: Sock picking

anchor.snippet: the minimum number of pulled socks required to guarantee a pair of the same color

annotations.note: The worst-case sock-count example is not formalized; it is a direct application of the pigeonhole principle.

annotations.label: Handshake degree pair

anchor.snippet: there is always a pair of people who will shake hands with the same number of people

annotations.note: The graph-theoretic corollary (two vertices of equal degree) is not formalized in Mathlib's SimpleGraph; only the underlying pigeonhole infrastructure exists.

annotations.label: Graph vertices share degree

anchor.snippet: there is at least one pair of vertices that share the same degree

annotations.note: No Mathlib theorem states that a finite graph with ≥2 vertices has two equal-degree vertices; only the pigeonhole principle it relies on is present.

annotations.label: Hair counting in London

anchor.snippet: there must be at least two people in London with the same number of hairs on their heads

annotations.note: Illustrative example not separately formalized; it is an application of the finite pigeonhole principle.

annotations.label: Birthday problem (367 people)

anchor.snippet: among 367 people, there is at least one pair of people who share the same birthday with 100% probability

annotations.note: The deterministic 367-people statement is a pigeonhole invocation (366 possible birthdays); the probabilistic 23/365 birthday problem is separately in Archive/Wiedijk100Theorems/BirthdayProblem.lean.

annotations.label: Team tournament

anchor.snippet: there must be at least one team featuring at least two of the seven players

annotations.note: Specific tournament example not formalized; it is a direct application of the pigeonhole principle.

anchors.snippet: there must be at least one team featuring at least two of the seven players

annotations.label: Subset sum to 10

anchor.snippet: must contain two elements whose sum is 10

annotations.note: This combinatorial example is not formalized; it is an application of the Finset pigeonhole principle to the pairing partition.

annotations.label: Hashing collisions

anchor.snippet: hashing those objects is no guarantee of uniqueness

annotations.note: The hashing-collision observation is not formalized; it is an informal application of the pigeonhole principle.

annotations.label: Lossless compression bound

anchor.snippet: will also make some other inputs larger

annotations.note: The counting argument that lossless compression cannot shrink all inputs is not formalized; it follows from non-existence of injections into a smaller finite set.

annotations.label: Density of fractional parts

anchor.snippet: of fractional parts is dense in

annotations.note: Verified: the multiples of a are dense on the circle ℝ/pℤ iff a/p is irrational, generalizing density of fractional parts {nα}.

annotations.label: Pumping lemma version (infinite/finite)

anchor.snippet: then two objects share a box

annotations.note: Verified: infinitely many pigeons in finitely many holes forces two distinct pigeons into the same hole.

annotations.label: Fisk's converse

anchor.snippet: then there is a box containing at most

annotations.note: Verified: dual pigeonhole giving a box with at most the floor of the average (card α ≤ card β * n ⟹ some fiber ≤ n).

annotations.label: Formulation 1 (at least two objects)

anchor.snippet: then some place receives at least two objects

annotations.note: Verified: if #t < #s and f maps s into t then two distinct elements of s collide, i.e. some box gets ≥2.

annotations.label: Formulation 2 (exactly one each)

anchor.snippet: no place receives more than one object

annotations.note: Verified: an injective placement (no box receives more than one) requires card α ≤ card β.

annotations.label: Formulation 3 (no injective function)

anchor.snippet: then there is no injective function from S to T

annotations.note: Verified: if card β < card α there is no embedding (injective map) α ↪ β.

annotations.label: Formulation 4 (some place empty)

anchor.snippet: then some place receives no object

annotations.note: Verified via contrapositive: a surjective placement needs card β ≤ card α, so with fewer objects than places the map is not surjective (some place empty).

annotations.label: Formulation 5 (no empty place)

anchor.snippet: no place receives no object

annotations.note: Verified: a surjective placement (no place left empty) requires card β ≤ card α.

annotations.label: Formulation 6 (no surjective function)

anchor.snippet: then there is no surjective function from S to T

annotations.note: Verified via contrapositive: if card S < card T no surjection S → T exists since surjectivity forces card T ≤ card S.

annotations.label: Strong form of pigeonhole principle

anchor.snippet: either the first box contains at least q 1 objects

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the uniform-threshold strong form; the general version with distinct thresholds q₁,…,qₙ is not present.

annotations.label: Quantified version (r or more objects)

anchor.snippet: then at least one of the boxes contains r or more of the objects

annotations.note: Verified: if card β * n ≤ card α then some fiber has at least n elements.

annotations.label: Ceiling lower bound restatement

anchor.snippet: then at least one container must hold at least

annotations.note: Verified: docstring states this gives a pigeonhole with at least the ceiling of the average number of pigeons.

annotations.label: Floor upper bound restatement

anchor.snippet: at least one container must hold no more than

annotations.note: Verified: gives a pigeonhole with at most the floor of the average number of pigeons (the minimum-is-at-most-the-mean form).

annotations.label: Probabilistic generalization

anchor.snippet: at least one pigeonhole will hold more than one pigeon with probability

annotations.note: Mathlib has a measure-space pigeonhole (overlapping sets when measures exceed the whole), but the explicit collision-probability formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Mean-based generalization

anchor.snippet: the probability is nonzero that X is greater than or equal to

annotations.note: The deterministic 'maximum is at least the mean' pigeonhole is formalized, but the probabilistic phrasing (P(X ≥ mean) > 0) is not.

annotations.label: Infinite cardinal injection

anchor.snippet: then there is no injection from A to B

annotations.note: Verified: #α ≤ #β ↔ Nonempty (α ↪ β), whose contrapositive gives no injection A → B when #A > #B.

annotations.label: Dedekind finite phrasing

anchor.snippet: then no surjection from A to B is injective

annotations.note: Mathlib has the finite injective-iff-surjective equivalences capturing Dedekind-finiteness, but not this exact compound phrasing about non-injective surjections.

annotations.label: Uncountable pigeonhole principle

anchor.snippet: there will exist at least one pigeonhole having uncountably many pigeons stuffed into it

annotations.note: Verified: for f with uncountable domain whose cardinality exceeds the codomain's, some fiber is uncountable.

annotations.label: Technical infinite restatement

anchor.snippet: then there exists an element b of B such that there exists a bijection between the preimage of b and A

annotations.note: Verified: under the cofinality hypothesis there exists a : α with #(f ⁻¹' {a}) = #β, i.e. the fiber is equinumerous with the domain.
